howItWorks.intro: This generator fuel runtime calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Runtime
rules.explanation: Tank capacity divided by fuel consumption rate at current load.

howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: How long will a generator run?
faq.answer: A typical 3500W generator runs 8-12 hours on 5 gallons at 50% load.

faq.question: Should I run at full load?
faq.answer: No, running at 50-75% load is more fuel efficient and extends generator life.

faq.question: Is the Generator Fuel Runtime Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard energy and utilities formulas trusted by homeowners and businesses. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What tank size should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate tank size value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Generator Fuel Runtime Calculator?
sections.body: The Generator Fuel Runtime Calculator is a free online tool designed for homeowners and businesses who need quick, accurate calculations in the energy and utilities space. By entering your tank size, rated watts, load percentage, you get instant results including estimated runtime, fuel consumption, cost per hour. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated runtime right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In energy and utilities, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by homeowners and businesses worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage energy costs with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your tank size and need to find the right estimated runtime. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Tank Size (gallons)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your tank size in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Rated Watts
howToSteps.description: Type or select your rated watts in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Load Percentage (%)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your load percentage in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your estimated runtime, fuel consumption, cost per hour. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your tank size before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both estimated runtime and fuel consumption — they work together to give you the full picture.

If you're unsure about your load percentage, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
